description: Validate that the stg_employees table in BigQuery contains exactly 14 columns as defined in the ICD.
precondition: The stg_employees table must exist in the target BigQuery dataset before test execution.
steps: 1. Connect to BigQuery target environment. 2. Execute the column count query against INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS filtered to stg_employees. 3. Compare returned column_count to the ICD-defined count of 14. 4. Fail and log if column_count differs from 14.
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS column_count FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name='stg_employees' AND table_schema='<dataset>'
sql_hint: COUNT(*) AS column_count FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND table_schema = '<dataset>'
linked_ac: Schema compatibility is validated before each load execution and mismatches are logged
resolved_query: COUNT(columns) from INFORMATION_SCHEMA: stg_employees vs stg_employees

description: Validate that no columns exist in the BigQuery stg_employees table that are not defined in the ICD schema.
precondition: The stg_employees table must exist in the target BigQuery dataset and the full ICD column list must be available as the reference set before test execution.
steps: 1. Connect to BigQuery target environment. 2. Execute the mismatch count query against INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS using the NOT IN ICD column list. 3. Compare returned mismatch_count to expected value of 0. 4. If mismatch_count > 0, log the offending column names and block load completion.
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS mismatch_count FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name='stg_employees' AND table_schema='<dataset>' AND column_name NOT IN ('EMPLOYEE_ID','FIRST_NAME','LAST_NAME','EMAIL','PHONE_NUMBER','HIRE_DATE','JOB_ID','SALARY','COMMISSION_PCT','MANAGER_ID','DEPARTMENT_ID','STATUS','CREATED_DATE','UPDATED_DATE')
sql_hint: COUNT(*) AS mismatch_count FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND table_schema = '<dataset>' AND column_name NOT IN (<14 ICD column names>)
linked_ac: Schema compatibility is validated before each load execution and mismatches are logged; New columns appearing in MySQL are handled per a documented predefined strategy before load proceeds
resolved_query: INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS: column names + data types stg_employees vs stg_employees
actual_result: All 14 columns match — names and data types identical

description: Validate that no NULL values exist in EMPLOYEE_ID after load, as it is the primary key and explicitly NOT NULL in the source system.
precondition: The stg_employees table must have completed at least one full load from the MySQL source prior to test execution.
steps: 1. Execute the validation query against <project>.<dataset>.stg_employees  2. Capture the null_count value returned  3. Assert null_count equals 0; if non-zero, block load and raise a data quality violation
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM <project>.<dataset>.stg_employees WHERE EMPLOYEE_ID IS NULL
sql_hint: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM stg_employees WHERE EMPLOYEE_ID IS NULL
expected_result: null_count = 0; zero NULL values in EMPLOYEE_ID
linked_ac: AC-8: Null constraint violations block load completion before data reaches BigQuery
resolved_query: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE `EMPLOYEE_ID` IS NULL — stg_employees vs stg_employees

test_name: Empty string check on EMAIL column
description: Validate that no empty string or whitespace-only values exist in EMAIL, which is marked NOT NULL in the MySQL source schema.
precondition: The stg_employees table must be populated via a completed full or incremental load, and EMAIL must be defined as NOT NULL in the source before test execution.
steps: 1. Execute the validation query against <project>.<dataset>.stg_employees  2. Capture the empty_count value returned  3. Assert empty_count equals 0; if non-zero, log violation and block load acceptance
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS empty_count FROM <project>.<dataset>.stg_employees WHERE EMAIL IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(EMAIL) = ''
sql_hint: SELECT COUNT(*) AS empty_count FROM stg_employees WHERE EMAIL IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(EMAIL) = ''
expected_result: empty_count = 0; no empty or whitespace-only EMAIL values present
linked_ac: AC-8: Null constraint violations block load completion before data reaches BigQuery
resolved_query: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE TRIM(CAST(`EMAIL` AS STRING)) = '' — stg_employees vs stg_employees
